model.label: DeepSeek V3.2
model.description: DeepSeek • General-purpose V-series model optimized for reasoning, coding, and efficient inference.

pick.rationale: Texas Tech's more balanced profile, featuring a top‑10 offense and a top‑30 defense, gives them a slight edge over Alabama's elite but one‑dimensional attack. The Red Raiders' ability to get timely stops should prove decisive in what figures to be a high‑scoring affair, while their own efficient offense can exploit the Crimson Tide's defensive vulnerabilities. Although Alabama's offense is formidable, Texas Tech's two‑way strength and Big‑12‑tested resilience make them the pick to advance.

reasoningStep.title: Analysis of Texas Tech vs Alabama
reasoningStep.summary: Evaluated efficiency ratings, matchup dynamics, and balance between offense and defense. Texas Tech's superior defensive efficiency and balanced profile provide a narrow edge over Alabama's elite offense but weaker defense.
evidence: Torvik ratings: Texas Tech rank 12 (adjoe 126.3, rank 9; adjde 98.8, rank 27). Alabama rank 17 (adjoe 129.4, rank 3; adjde 102.8, rank 67). KenPom ratings: Texas Tech rank 19 (adj_o 125.1, rank 12); Alabama rank 16 (adj_o 129, rank 3). Texas Tech holds a significant defensive advantage while maintaining a top‑10 offense, creating a more balanced team profile than Alabama's offense‑heavy approach.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

Prior committed picks: 49

Interesting. Alabama has an elite offense (ranked 3rd in both Torvik and KenPom) but a weaker defense (67th in Torvik). Texas Tech has a very good offense (9th Torvik, 12th KenPom) and a much better defense (27th Torvik, 19th KenPom?). Actually KenPom defense rank: 99.9 adjusted D, but need to see rank. The data shows adj_d: 99.9, but not rank. However, based on Torvik, Texas Tech has a clear defensive advantage. Both teams have similar overall rankings: Alabama 17/16, Texas Tech 12/19. Texas Tech has a slightly better overall ranking in Torvik (12 vs 17) and similar in KenPom (19 vs 16). Let me search for recent news, injuries, and matchup analysis.

Alabama: adjoe 129.4 (rank 3), adjde 102.8 (rank 67). That's a huge offensive efficiency but mediocre defense.
Texas Tech: adjoe 126.3 (rank 9), adjde 98.8 (rank 27). Very good offense, solid defense.

Texas Tech: adj_o 125.1 (rank 12), adj_d 99.9 (likely around 30-40).

Overall, Texas Tech appears more balanced. The matchup could be a high-scoring affair, but Texas Tech's defense could be the difference. However, Alabama's offense is elite and could overcome defensive deficiencies. I should also consider the style: Alabama plays fast, high-tempo, three-point heavy. Texas Tech is also offensive-minded but more balanced. The game could come down to whether Texas Tech can slow down Alabama's offense. Texas Tech's defense is good but not elite (rank 27). However, that's still much better than Alabama's defense.

modelTrace.content:
All web searches are failing. This is a limitation. I'll have to proceed with the data I have.

Now, I need to decide on a winner. This is a classic 4 vs 5 matchup, very close. According to efficiency margins, Texas Tech has a slightly better overall rating (Torvik rank 12 vs 17). KenPom has Alabama slightly higher (16 vs 19). Essentially even.

But I should consider conference strength: Big 12 is typically stronger than SEC, but both are power conferences. Texas Tech played in a tougher conference (Big 12) and still managed 22-10 record. Alabama 23-9 in SEC.

I think Texas Tech's defensive advantage could be key. Alabama's poor defense may allow Texas Tech to score efficiently. However, Alabama's offense is so good that they can outscore opponents. In a shootout, I'd lean towards Alabama. But Texas Tech's defense might be good enough to slow them down a bit.

Let me calculate approximate adjusted efficiency margins: Torvik adjoe minus adjde gives net efficiency.

Alabama: 129.4 - 102.8 = +26.6
Texas Tech: 126.3 - 98.8 = +27.5

Texas Tech has a slightly higher net efficiency. That suggests Texas Tech is marginally better.

KenPom: Alabama adj_em 25.64, Texas Tech 25.19. Alabama slightly higher. So metrics are contradictory.

I need to look at other factors like tempo, three-point defense, etc. But I don't have that data.

I should also consider that Alabama is a 4 seed, Texas Tech 5 seed, so Alabama is slightly favored by seeding. But efficiency ratings suggest they are even.

In my prior picks, I had confidence High for both teams in the first round. Now I need to choose.

I'll think about matchup specifics: Alabama's weak defense vs Texas Tech's strong offense. Texas Tech's decent defense vs Alabama's elite offense. Which team can exploit the other's weakness more? Alabama's defense is weaker (rank 67) than Texas Tech's defense (rank 27). So Texas Tech's offense may have an easier time scoring than Alabama's offense against Texas Tech's defense. That could give Texas Tech an edge.

Also, Texas Tech's defense is good enough to potentially limit Alabama's offense somewhat. So I'll pick Texas Tech with Medium confidence.

But I need to consider upset risk: The underdog is Texas Tech (5 seed) vs Alabama (4 seed). Actually, Texas Tech is the lower seed, so they are the underdog. I need to consider if Texas Tech has a credible path to win. Yes, they have balanced metrics and better defense. So picking Texas Tech is not a huge upset.

Alternatively, I could pick Alabama because of their elite offense. Many analysts say "defense wins championships," but in a single game, offense can carry.

Let me check if there is any head-to-head history. Not available.

I'll make a decision: Texas Tech with Medium confidence. Rationale: Texas Tech's more balanced profile, with a top-10 offense and top-30 defense, gives them an edge over Alabama's elite but one-dimensional attack. The Red Raiders' ability to get stops when needed should prove decisive in a high-scoring affair, while their own efficient offense can exploit the Crimson Tide's defensive vulnerabilities.
